commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram `threadReplies: "auto"` as the default, threads DM sessions when `getMe.has_topics_enabled` is true, refreshes startup bot info for auto mode, and updates Telegram docs, config metadata, and tests.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +186, Docs +1, Generated 0. Total +266 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not from live runtime: current main defaults omitted `threadReplies` to `off`, and the linked issue describes topic-enabled DMs needing capability-based split sessions. I did not reproduce this with a real Telegram bot.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is maintainer judgment on the compatibility/default change plus contributor live Telegram proof, not a narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: fix(telegram): auto-detect DM thread sessions
target.body:
Fixes #86513

## Summary

- Add `auto` as the Telegram DM `threadReplies` default and expose it in schema, generated metadata, docs, and UI hints.
- Use fresh `getMe.has_topics_enabled` capability data for auto DM topic routing, bypassing stale startup bot-info cache when auto is active.
- Route inbound messages, native commands, plugin commands, callbacks, and session-state lookups through DM topic sessions when auto or explicit config requires it.
- Preserve bound route session keys for auto-default routing while still honoring explicit `inbound`, `always`, `requireTopic`, and topic config isolation.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.thread-binding.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.gateway.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/telegram.md extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.test-harness.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.thread-binding.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.gateway.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-ui-hints.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts src/config/types.telegram.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram DM messages with `message_thread_id` now use topic-scoped sessions by default when the bot reports `has_topics_enabled=true`.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree with mocked Telegram handler/runtime tests; no live Telegram bot account used.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused Vitest wrapper command above after the final routing changes and formatter pass.
Evidence after fix: 7 test files passed, 261 tests passed; generated config checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: Auto uses topic sessions only for unbound/default routes, bound auto routes keep their bound session, and explicit thread config still isolates topics on bound routes.
What was not tested: Live Telegram bot behavior and Crabbox/Testbox proof; Crabbox was blocked because the local `crabbox` binary failed/missing sanity checks.
